title: Review the recent x-auto runtime change for short note-lead exemption in /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto. Focus on approval_guard.py and the newly added/modified tests around note leads. Look for correctness holes, regressions, or overly broad exemption logic. Do not modify files; report concise findings...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: primary-source enforcement is bypassed for any post containing `note.com/`, `x.com/`, or `twitter.com/` in `text`. Files: [approval_guard.py#L65](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L65), [approval_guard.py#L84](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L84). Why: `can_approve()` returns early on `_is_thumbnail_exempt()` before running quant/research source checks. Suggested fix: always run source-claim checks; only skip thumbnail/image checks for exempt types. Add tests for “note-link + quant claim + missing/non-primary source”. 2. Medium: short note-lead exemption is overly broad. Files: [approval_guard.py#L34](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L34), [approval_guard.py#L42](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L42). Why: `_is_note_lead_post()` is just substring match (`"note.com/" in text`), so any short post with an incidental note URL bypasses the 800-char gate. Suggested fix: tighten to a note-lead pattern (e.g., single note URL + short lead template) instead of raw substring. 3. Medium: text-gate cutoff logic is fail-open on malformed/missing `created_at`. Files: [approval_guard.py#L43](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py#L43). Why: string comparison (`created_at >= "2026-03-30"`) is brittle and skips gate when `created_at` is empty/non-ISO. Suggested fix: parse datetime explicitly; on parse failure, either block with a clear reason or apply conservative gate. 4. Regression/inconsistency: newly added “short note allowed” tests in apply/prepare paths conflict with current runtime gate order. Files: [prepare_post_bundle.py#L162](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/prepare_post_bundle.py#L162), [test_prepare_post_bundle.py#L232](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_prepare_post_bundle.py#L232), [test_apply_post_package.py#L192](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_apply_post_package.py#L192). Why: `_scheduler_gate_notes()` adds `text length ... below scheduler gate` before note exemption, so short note leads remain blocked there. Suggested fix: reuse `approval_guard.text_gate_blockers()` as SSOT or move note exemption check before the local length check. I did not modify files. I also ran `pytest -q tests/test_approval_guard.py` (passes), and spot-checked behavior with direct `can_approve()` calls showing the bypass in finding #1.
